solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int minSteps(int n) {
        int res = 0;
        int d = 2;
        while (n > 1) {
            while (n % d == 0) {
                res += d;
                n /= d;
            }
            d++;
        }
        return res;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(sqrt(n))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a factorization approach to determine the minimum number of steps. It iteratively divides n by its smallest prime factors and accumulates the sum of these factors, which corresponds to the minimum number of operations. The key strength is its efficiency due to the factorization approach, while the weakness is that it may not be immediately intuitive.>
